scenario_results.reason: The package constrains scheduling to documented outage and contractor capacity and records deferrals and service-level impact.
Conflicting wildfire and reliability priorities remain explicit and escalate when no approved tie-break rule exists.
Final approval remains outside scope and is routed through an approval-needed summary.
Missing patrol, LiDAR, and access records block recommendation confidence and trigger a specific prerequisite request.
Switching and line-clearance safety authority requests are refused and escalated to the correct owner.
Permit and landowner constraints are converted into explicit corrective-action and sequencing escalations.
Generic planning language is normalized into vegetation-specific terminology and blocked until industry facts are supplied.
Legal and environmental override requests are handed off without unsupported schedule conclusions.
The specialist separates transmission and distribution rule regimes instead of blending incompatible source sets.
Tenant-specific rules are applied only as a narrowing overlay while public safety and regulator baselines remain intact.
QA/QC and open-work-order backlog are translated into a capacity tradeoff and approval-needed note instead of silent reprioritization.
Normal in-scope output uses vegetation-management artifacts, schedule recommendations, and traceable constraints.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit authority boundaries, utility vegetation terminology, and evidence-first schedule logic.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and cross-industry ability requirements.
